name: danantara-way-governance
description: Applies the Danantara Way doctrines—commercial discipline, national consequence, active ownership, prudence, transparency, measurable transformation, sovereignty, and intergenerational value. Use when framing, assessing, challenging, or approving any material Danantara decision.
purpose: Applies the Danantara Way doctrines—commercial discipline, national consequence, active ownership, prudence, transparency, measurable transformation, sovereignty, and intergenerational value.

name: danantara-ai-constitution
description: Enforces enterprise AI principles, authority boundaries, confidentiality, evidence standards, human accountability, prohibited actions, and escalation rules. Use for every AI-enabled workflow that handles Danantara information, analysis, recommendations, or decisions.
purpose: Enforces enterprise AI principles, authority boundaries, confidentiality, evidence standards, human accountability, prohibited actions, and escalation rules. Use for every AI-enabled workflow that handles Danantara information, analysis, recommendations, or decisions.

name: mandate-authority-interpreter
description: Interprets the applicable mandate, legal authority, delegated power, and institutional boundary for a proposed action. Use when responsibility among BPI, DAM, DIM, DDMF, BUMN, boards, committees, or government stakeholders is unclear.
purpose: Interprets the applicable mandate, legal authority, delegated power, and institutional boundary for a proposed action.

name: decision-rights-checker
description: Identifies who proposes, reviews, recommends, approves, executes, monitors, and bears accountability for a decision. Use before issuing recommendations, commitments, capital allocations, corporate actions, disclosures, or binding instructions.
purpose: Identifies who proposes, reviews, recommends, approves, executes, monitors, and bears accountability for a decision. Use before issuing recommendations, commitments, capital allocations, corporate actions, disclosures, or binding instructions.

name: conflict-of-interest-gate
description: Detects actual, potential, and perceived conflicts of interest, related parties, hidden beneficiaries, influence channels, and recusal needs. Use for appointments, procurement, investments, partnerships, restructuring, transactions, and material exceptions.
purpose: Detects actual, potential, and perceived conflicts of interest, related parties, hidden beneficiaries, influence channels, and recusal needs. Use for appointments, procurement, investments, partnerships, restructuring, transactions, and material exceptions.

name: prudence-and-downside-gate
description: Tests capital preservation, permanent-loss risk, downside scenarios, reversibility, liquidity, and risk ownership. Use before committing capital, extending support, changing strategy, accepting guarantees, or approving high-uncertainty initiatives.
purpose: Tests capital preservation, permanent-loss risk, downside scenarios, reversibility, liquidity, and risk ownership. Use before committing capital, extending support, changing strategy, accepting guarantees, or approving high-uncertainty initiatives.

name: transparency-disclosure-gate
description: Determines what material information, assumptions, risks, decisions, and performance must be documented or disclosed while preserving lawful confidentiality. Use for board papers, investor materials, public statements, financial reporting, and major decisions.
purpose: Determines what material information, assumptions, risks, decisions, and performance must be documented or disclosed while preserving lawful confidentiality. Use for board papers, investor materials, public statements, financial reporting, and major decisions.

name: strategic-confidentiality-classifier
description: Classifies information by sensitivity, strategic importance, legal restriction, market impact, personal data, and national-security relevance. Use before retrieval, sharing, model processing, cross-border transfer, publication, or external collaboration.
purpose: Classifies information by sensitivity, strategic importance, legal restriction, market impact, personal data, and national-security relevance. Use before retrieval, sharing, model processing, cross-border transfer, publication, or external collaboration.

name: integrity-beneficiary-check
description: Tests whether a proposal is lawful, ethical, independently supportable, and free from concealed beneficiaries, side arrangements, or improper advantage. Use when a transaction or instruction appears formally compliant but substantively questionable.
purpose: Tests whether a proposal is lawful, ethical, independently supportable, and free from concealed beneficiaries, side arrangements, or improper advantage.

name: long-term-value-test
description: Assesses whether a decision creates durable economic, strategic, capability, resilience, and intergenerational value over 10–20 years. Use when short-term earnings, political urgency, or temporary market conditions could distort the decision.
purpose: Assesses whether a decision creates durable economic, strategic, capability, resilience, and intergenerational value over 10–20 years.

name: dual-mandate-balancer
description: Balances commercially disciplined return with measurable national impact without disguising subsidy, bailout, or weak economics. Use when return, resilience, employment, sovereignty, industrialization, or public-service outcomes must be evaluated together.
purpose: Balances commercially disciplined return with measurable national impact without disguising subsidy, bailout, or weak economics.

name: vocabulary-and-narrative-guard
description: Enforces Danantara preferred vocabulary and flags unsupported phrases, vague synergies, informal-instruction logic, unmeasured national impact, bailout framing, and narrative-only decisions. Use when drafting or reviewing any formal Danantara output.
purpose: Enforces Danantara preferred vocabulary and flags unsupported phrases, vague synergies, informal-instruction logic, unmeasured national impact, bailout framing, and narrative-only decisions.

name: danantara-master-orchestrator
description: Routes complex Danantara requests across governance, DAM, DIM, DDMF, functional, sector, and assurance skills; sequences evidence, analysis, challenge, approval, and audit steps. Use for multi-domain, material, ambiguous, or end-to-end workflows.
purpose: Routes complex Danantara requests across governance, DAM, DIM, DDMF, functional, sector, and assurance skills; sequences evidence, analysis, challenge, approval, and audit steps. Use for multi-domain, material, ambiguous, or end-to-end workflows.

name: intent-and-entity-router
description: Classifies user intent, affected entity, decision type, process stage, materiality, sensitivity, and required skill family. Use as the first routing step for requests that may involve BPI, DAM, DIM, DDMF, BUMN, or multiple functions.
purpose: Classifies user intent, affected entity, decision type, process stage, materiality, sensitivity, and required skill family. Use as the first routing step for requests that may involve BPI, DAM, DIM, DDMF, BUMN, or multiple functions.

name: workflow-planner
description: Converts an objective into ordered tasks, dependencies, evidence needs, specialist roles, challenge gates, approvals, and deliverables. Use when work requires multiple analytical steps or coordinated human and AI participation.
purpose: Converts an objective into ordered tasks, dependencies, evidence needs, specialist roles, challenge gates, approvals, and deliverables.

name: skill-chain-composer
description: Selects and sequences reusable skills into an end-to-end workflow while avoiding duplicated analysis and conflicting mandates. Use after routing when a request requires several domain, sector, governance, or assurance capabilities.
purpose: Selects and sequences reusable skills into an end-to-end workflow while avoiding duplicated analysis and conflicting mandates. Use after routing when a request requires several domain, sector, governance, or assurance capabilities.

name: parallel-analysis-dispatcher
description: Dispatches independent workstreams in parallel, defines non-overlapping scopes, and reconciles results. Use for due diligence, portfolio reviews, scenario studies, board preparation, or time-critical analysis requiring multiple specialists.
purpose: Dispatches independent workstreams in parallel, defines non-overlapping scopes, and reconciles results. Use for due diligence, portfolio reviews, scenario studies, board preparation, or time-critical analysis requiring multiple specialists.

name: evidence-retrieval-orchestrator
description: Plans and coordinates retrieval from approved internal and external sources, prioritizing authoritative, current, traceable evidence. Use when decisions require documents, data, benchmarks, regulations, market intelligence, or historical precedents.
purpose: Plans and coordinates retrieval from approved internal and external sources, prioritizing authoritative, current, traceable evidence.

name: source-hierarchy-resolver
description: Ranks conflicting sources by legal authority, ownership, recency, audit status, independence, and relevance; records unresolved discrepancies. Use when data, policies, valuations, or narratives disagree.
purpose: Ranks conflicting sources by legal authority, ownership, recency, audit status, independence, and relevance; records unresolved discrepancies.

name: data-classification-router
description: Routes information and tasks only to models, tools, repositories, locations, and users permitted by the applicable data classification. Use before any AI processing, connector call, cross-entity sharing, or external model use.
purpose: Routes information and tasks only to models, tools, repositories, locations, and users permitted by the applicable data classification. Use before any AI processing, connector call, cross-entity sharing, or external model use.

name: human-approval-orchestrator
description: Maps risk-tiered outputs to required reviewers, approvers, recusals, quorum, conditions, and evidence of approval. Use whenever AI analysis may influence capital, governance, legal, personnel, disclosure, or strategic decisions.
purpose: Maps risk-tiered outputs to required reviewers, approvers, recusals, quorum, conditions, and evidence of approval.

name: red-team-orchestrator
description: Assigns independent challengers to test thesis, valuation, assumptions, legal basis, governance, impact, execution, and downside without sharing the preferred answer. Use before material recommendations or committee decisions.
purpose: Assigns independent challengers to test thesis, valuation, assumptions, legal basis, governance, impact, execution, and downside without sharing the preferred answer. Use before material recommendations or committee decisions.

name: assumption-uncertainty-manager
description: Registers assumptions, confidence, data gaps, sensitivities, disputed facts, and conditions that would change the recommendation. Use in forecasts, valuations, scenarios, due diligence, strategic plans, and incomplete-information decisions.
purpose: Registers assumptions, confidence, data gaps, sensitivities, disputed facts, and conditions that would change the recommendation. Use in forecasts, valuations, scenarios, due diligence, strategic plans, and incomplete-information decisions.

name: tension-resolution-engine
description: Frames and tests Danantara's core tensions: commercial return versus national mandate, independence versus direction, long-term value versus short-term intervention, transformation capital versus bailout, and transparency versus confidentiality. Use when objectives conflict.
purpose: Frames and tests Danantara's core tensions: commercial return versus national mandate, independence versus direction, long-term value versus short-term intervention, transformation capital versus bailout, and transparency versus confidentiality.

name: output-assembly-orchestrator
description: Integrates specialist outputs into one decision-ready product with recommendation, alternatives, evidence, risks, dissent, approvals, and next actions. Use after multi-skill analysis or parallel workstreams.
purpose: Integrates specialist outputs into one decision-ready product with recommendation, alternatives, evidence, risks, dissent, approvals, and next actions. Use after multi-skill analysis or parallel workstreams.

name: workflow-state-manager
description: Tracks workflow stage, completed evidence, open issues, dependencies, decisions, approvals, deadlines, and handoffs. Use for long-running investments, transformations, restructurings, projects, and recurring portfolio reviews.
purpose: Tracks workflow stage, completed evidence, open issues, dependencies, decisions, approvals, deadlines, and handoffs. Use for long-running investments, transformations, restructurings, projects, and recurring portfolio reviews.

name: escalation-and-exception-router
description: Escalates unresolved conflicts, missing authority, inadequate evidence, policy exceptions, critical risks, ethical concerns, or prohibited actions to the correct human body. Use whenever standard workflow cannot safely continue.
purpose: Escalates unresolved conflicts, missing authority, inadequate evidence, policy exceptions, critical risks, ethical concerns, or prohibited actions to the correct human body.

name: policy-knowledge-curator
description: Curates approved policies, manuals, delegations, standards, templates, effective dates, superseded versions, and owners into a controlled knowledge base. Use when building or updating authoritative Danantara policy context.
purpose: Curates approved policies, manuals, delegations, standards, templates, effective dates, superseded versions, and owners into a controlled knowledge base.

name: legal-regulatory-monitor
description: Monitors applicable laws, regulations, decisions, standards, and official guidance; maps changes to affected entities, processes, documents, and controls. Use for regulatory horizon scanning and change-impact assessment.
purpose: Monitors applicable laws, regulations, decisions, standards, and official guidance; maps changes to affected entities, processes, documents, and controls. Use for regulatory horizon scanning and change-impact assessment.

name: portfolio-knowledge-graph
description: Builds and queries relationships among entities, assets, ownership, boards, contracts, lenders, projects, risks, KPIs, transactions, and stakeholders. Use when analysis depends on cross-portfolio context or hidden dependencies.
purpose: Builds and queries relationships among entities, assets, ownership, boards, contracts, lenders, projects, risks, KPIs, transactions, and stakeholders.

name: decision-precedent-retriever
description: Retrieves comparable prior decisions, their rationale, assumptions, approvals, outcomes, and lessons without treating precedent as automatic authority. Use for board, investment, restructuring, policy, and exception decisions.
purpose: Retrieves comparable prior decisions, their rationale, assumptions, approvals, outcomes, and lessons without treating precedent as automatic authority. Use for board, investment, restructuring, policy, and exception decisions.

name: benchmark-intelligence-manager
description: Selects relevant global, regional, sector, sovereign-fund, and peer-company benchmarks; normalizes definitions and explains comparability limits. Use for performance targets, valuation, operating models, governance, and transformation ambition.
purpose: Selects relevant global, regional, sector, sovereign-fund, and peer-company benchmarks; normalizes definitions and explains comparability limits. Use for performance targets, valuation, operating models, governance, and transformation ambition.

name: data-quality-and-lineage-checker
description: Tests completeness, accuracy, consistency, timeliness, ownership, reconciliation, transformations, and source lineage of decision data. Use before models, dashboards, consolidated reporting, valuations, and material recommendations.
purpose: Tests completeness, accuracy, consistency, timeliness, ownership, reconciliation, transformations, and source lineage of decision data. Use before models, dashboards, consolidated reporting, valuations, and material recommendations.

name: document-evidence-extractor
description: Extracts structured facts, obligations, assumptions, dates, financial terms, approvals, risks, and citations from documents while preserving page-level traceability. Use for contracts, board papers, reports, policies, studies, and data rooms.
purpose: Extracts structured facts, obligations, assumptions, dates, financial terms, approvals, risks, and citations from documents while preserving page-level traceability. Use for contracts, board papers, reports, policies, studies, and data rooms.

name: institutional-memory-capture
description: Converts completed decisions, projects, negotiations, transformations, and incidents into reusable lessons, patterns, controls, and precedents. Use at milestones, post-mortems, leadership transitions, and workflow closure.
purpose: Converts completed decisions, projects, negotiations, transformations, and incidents into reusable lessons, patterns, controls, and precedents. Use at milestones, post-mortems, leadership transitions, and workflow closure.

name: national-priority-alignment
description: Maps proposals to formal national priorities, economic-transformation objectives, measurable outcomes, and responsible institutions. Use when a project or investment claims strategic-national relevance.
purpose: Maps proposals to formal national priorities, economic-transformation objectives, measurable outcomes, and responsible institutions.

name: strategic-national-interest-test
description: Tests whether an action materially advances resilience, sovereignty, industrial capability, productivity, competitiveness, or long-term national value beyond ordinary commercial activity. Use for strategic designations and exceptional support.
purpose: Tests whether an action materially advances resilience, sovereignty, industrial capability, productivity, competitiveness, or long-term national value beyond ordinary commercial activity. Use for strategic designations and exceptional support.

name: enterprise-portfolio-synthesizer
description: Produces a consolidated view of value, performance, risk, capital, impact, transformation, and concentration across the Danantara ecosystem. Use for enterprise reviews, supervisory reporting, and strategic capital allocation.
purpose: Produces a consolidated view of value, performance, risk, capital, impact, transformation, and concentration across the Danantara ecosystem. Use for enterprise reviews, supervisory reporting, and strategic capital allocation.

name: dam-dim-ddmf-boundary-router
description: Determines whether an opportunity belongs primarily to active BUMN ownership, commercial investment, development finance, or coordinated treatment. Use when mandate overlap or funding-vehicle ambiguity exists.
purpose: Determines whether an opportunity belongs primarily to active BUMN ownership, commercial investment, development finance, or coordinated treatment.

name: cross-portfolio-synergy-finder
description: Identifies measurable revenue, cost, capital, capability, technology, supply-chain, market-access, and risk synergies across portfolio entities. Use when evaluating collaboration, shared services, consolidation, or ecosystem strategies.
purpose: Identifies measurable revenue, cost, capital, capability, technology, supply-chain, market-access, and risk synergies across portfolio entities.

name: transformation-agenda-orchestrator
description: Coordinates enterprise-wide transformation themes, target architecture, waves, accountable owners, dependencies, benefits, and intervention thresholds. Use for multi-BUMN or cross-holding transformation programs.
purpose: Coordinates enterprise-wide transformation themes, target architecture, waves, accountable owners, dependencies, benefits, and intervention thresholds. Use for multi-BUMN or cross-holding transformation programs.

name: national-resilience-assessor
description: Assesses exposure and response options for disruptions affecting energy, food, health, finance, digital systems, critical minerals, infrastructure, and strategic supply chains. Use for resilience investments and contingency decisions.
purpose: Assesses exposure and response options for disruptions affecting energy, food, health, finance, digital systems, critical minerals, infrastructure, and strategic supply chains. Use for resilience investments and contingency decisions.

name: institutional-trust-monitor
description: Evaluates how decisions, disclosures, governance, execution, and exceptions affect credibility, cost of capital, partner confidence, employee confidence, and public legitimacy. Use for sensitive or precedent-setting actions.
purpose: Evaluates how decisions, disclosures, governance, execution, and exceptions affect credibility, cost of capital, partner confidence, employee confidence, and public legitimacy. Use for sensitive or precedent-setting actions.

name: executive-board-brief-builder
description: Builds concise decision briefs with issue, recommendation, alternatives, evidence, scenarios, risks, dissent, decision rights, approvals, and action tracking. Use for executive, supervisory, board, or committee meetings.
purpose: Builds concise decision briefs with issue, recommendation, alternatives, evidence, scenarios, risks, dissent, decision rights, approvals, and action tracking. Use for executive, supervisory, board, or committee meetings.

name: public-accountability-packager
description: Translates material performance, investments, transformations, risks, and impact into transparent, consistent, auditable public-accountability materials without exposing protected information. Use for reports, hearings, and public communications.
purpose: Translates material performance, investments, transformations, risks, and impact into transparent, consistent, auditable public-accountability materials without exposing protected information. Use for reports, hearings, and public communications.

name: bumn-portfolio-segmenter
description: Segments BUMN and assets by strategic role, economics, competitiveness, maturity, risk, ownership intent, and intervention need. Use to determine retain, transform, consolidate, partner, divest, liquidate, or monitor pathways.
purpose: Segments BUMN and assets by strategic role, economics, competitiveness, maturity, risk, ownership intent, and intervention need. Use to determine retain, transform, consolidate, partner, divest, liquidate, or monitor pathways.

name: corporate-parenting-advantage
description: Tests whether Danantara can create more value through governance, capital, talent, market access, technology, risk management, or synergy than an entity could create independently. Use before ownership interventions or retention decisions.
purpose: Tests whether Danantara can create more value through governance, capital, talent, market access, technology, risk management, or synergy than an entity could create independently. Use before ownership interventions or retention decisions.

name: value-creation-plan-builder
description: Builds quantified value-creation plans linking baseline, levers, initiatives, owners, investment, milestones, KPIs, cash impact, risks, and exit criteria. Use after portfolio diagnosis or before approving transformation capital.
purpose: Builds quantified value-creation plans linking baseline, levers, initiatives, owners, investment, milestones, KPIs, cash impact, risks, and exit criteria. Use after portfolio diagnosis or before approving transformation capital.

name: operational-excellence-diagnostic
description: Diagnoses reliability, productivity, cost, quality, service, asset utilization, process waste, maintenance, supply chain, and execution discipline against relevant benchmarks. Use for performance improvement and turnaround design.
purpose: Diagnoses reliability, productivity, cost, quality, service, asset utilization, process waste, maintenance, supply chain, and execution discipline against relevant benchmarks. Use for performance improvement and turnaround design.

name: financial-performance-diagnostic
description: Analyzes earnings quality, cash conversion, balance sheet, liquidity, leverage, returns, capital intensity, dividend capacity, and value leakage. Use for portfolio reviews, interventions, restructuring, and capital decisions.
purpose: Analyzes earnings quality, cash conversion, balance sheet, liquidity, leverage, returns, capital intensity, dividend capacity, and value leakage. Use for portfolio reviews, interventions, restructuring, and capital decisions.

name: capital-allocation-reviewer
description: Reviews capex, opex, dividends, acquisitions, funding, and portfolio capital against strategy, hurdle rates, risk-adjusted return, alternatives, and opportunity cost. Use before approving material resource allocation.
purpose: Reviews capex, opex, dividends, acquisitions, funding, and portfolio capital against strategy, hurdle rates, risk-adjusted return, alternatives, and opportunity cost. Use before approving material resource allocation.

name: portfolio-rationalization-analyzer
description: Evaluates whether entities, subsidiaries, assets, and business lines should remain standalone, merge, consolidate, divest, liquidate, or form partnerships. Use when structure no longer matches economic or strategic logic.
purpose: Evaluates whether entities, subsidiaries, assets, and business lines should remain standalone, merge, consolidate, divest, liquidate, or form partnerships.

name: merger-consolidation-case
description: Builds the economic, strategic, governance, competition, operational, people, technology, and execution case for merger or consolidation. Use only when measurable parenting and synergy advantages exceed integration costs and risks.
purpose: Builds the economic, strategic, governance, competition, operational, people, technology, and execution case for merger or consolidation. Use only when measurable parenting and synergy advantages exceed integration costs and risks.

name: divestment-exit-readiness
description: Assesses strategic rationale, value, marketability, legal readiness, stakeholder impact, separation complexity, data-room readiness, and timing for divestment or exit. Use for non-core assets and rational ownership decisions.
purpose: Assesses strategic rationale, value, marketability, legal readiness, stakeholder impact, separation complexity, data-room readiness, and timing for divestment or exit. Use for non-core assets and rational ownership decisions.

name: turnaround-triage
description: Distinguishes temporary liquidity stress, operational underperformance, balance-sheet distress, governance failure, and structurally broken business models. Use at the first sign of material deterioration or support requests.
purpose: Distinguishes temporary liquidity stress, operational underperformance, balance-sheet distress, governance failure, and structurally broken business models. Use at the first sign of material deterioration or support requests.

name: restructuring-options-designer
description: Designs integrated strategic, operational, financial, organizational, governance, leadership, and portfolio restructuring options with scenarios and consequences. Use after turnaround diagnosis and before committing support.
purpose: Designs integrated strategic, operational, financial, organizational, governance, leadership, and portfolio restructuring options with scenarios and consequences. Use after turnaround diagnosis and before committing support.

name: conditional-capital-designer
description: Structures transformation capital with milestones, covenants, management accountability, drawdown gates, monitoring, consequences, and exit criteria. Use to prevent moral hazard and distinguish investable turnaround from bailout.
purpose: Structures transformation capital with milestones, covenants, management accountability, drawdown gates, monitoring, consequences, and exit criteria. Use to prevent moral hazard and distinguish investable turnaround from bailout.

name: management-accountability-contract
description: Translates transformation commitments into measurable management outcomes, decision rights, resources, milestones, incentives, consequences, and reporting. Use when leadership is accountable for turnaround or value creation.
purpose: Translates transformation commitments into measurable management outcomes, decision rights, resources, milestones, incentives, consequences, and reporting.

name: ceo-transformation-challenger
description: Challenges whether a CEO has an evidence-based diagnosis, credible destination, coherent transformation roadmap, capable team, resource plan, milestones, and accountability. Use for appointments, reviews, and major transformation programs.
purpose: Challenges whether a CEO has an evidence-based diagnosis, credible destination, coherent transformation roadmap, capable team, resource plan, milestones, and accountability. Use for appointments, reviews, and major transformation programs.

name: talent-meritocracy-assessor
description: Assesses whether critical roles are filled by people with the competence, integrity, track record, leadership capacity, and mission fit required for the problem. Use for board, executive, transformation, and succession decisions.
purpose: Assesses whether critical roles are filled by people with the competence, integrity, track record, leadership capacity, and mission fit required for the problem. Use for board, executive, transformation, and succession decisions.

name: enterprise-digital-synergy
description: Identifies duplicated applications, data, vendors, capabilities, and platforms; defines what should be shared, standardized, interoperable, federated, or retired. Use for cross-BUMN digital strategy and investment review.
purpose: Identifies duplicated applications, data, vendors, capabilities, and platforms; defines what should be shared, standardized, interoperable, federated, or retired. Use for cross-BUMN digital strategy and investment review.

name: shared-platform-architect
description: Designs reusable enterprise capabilities, data products, integration standards, identity, cybersecurity, cloud, AI, and operating models across portfolio companies. Use when common platforms can outperform fragmented applications.
purpose: Designs reusable enterprise capabilities, data products, integration standards, identity, cybersecurity, cloud, AI, and operating models across portfolio companies.

name: post-merger-integration-planner
description: Plans value capture, Day-1 continuity, governance, organization, talent, culture, processes, systems, data, controls, brand, and stakeholder integration after corporate consolidation. Use before closing and through stabilization.
purpose: Plans value capture, Day-1 continuity, governance, organization, talent, culture, processes, systems, data, controls, brand, and stakeholder integration after corporate consolidation. Use before closing and through stabilization.

name: investment-mandate-interpreter
description: Translates DIM's mandate into eligible assets, return objectives, risk appetite, impact expectations, geography, liquidity, horizon, concentration, exclusions, and approval requirements. Use before screening or designing an investment strategy.
purpose: Translates DIM's mandate into eligible assets, return objectives, risk appetite, impact expectations, geography, liquidity, horizon, concentration, exclusions, and approval requirements. Use before screening or designing an investment strategy.

name: investment-theme-generator
description: Develops evidence-based investment themes from national priorities, structural trends, market gaps, capabilities, resilience needs, and risk-adjusted opportunity. Use for strategy refresh, origination, and sector allocation.
purpose: Develops evidence-based investment themes from national priorities, structural trends, market gaps, capabilities, resilience needs, and risk-adjusted opportunity. Use for strategy refresh, origination, and sector allocation.

name: opportunity-screening
description: Screens opportunities for mandate fit, strategic relevance, commercial logic, readiness, sponsor quality, preliminary risk-return, additionality, impact, and fatal flaws. Use before allocating due-diligence resources.
purpose: Screens opportunities for mandate fit, strategic relevance, commercial logic, readiness, sponsor quality, preliminary risk-return, additionality, impact, and fatal flaws. Use before allocating due-diligence resources.

name: investment-thesis-builder
description: Builds a falsifiable investment thesis covering value drivers, variant perception, competitive advantage, catalysts, downside, national consequence, entry valuation, ownership plan, and exit logic. Use for shortlisted opportunities.
purpose: Builds a falsifiable investment thesis covering value drivers, variant perception, competitive advantage, catalysts, downside, national consequence, entry valuation, ownership plan, and exit logic. Use for shortlisted opportunities.

name: commercial-due-diligence
description: Tests market size, demand, customers, competition, pricing, business model, unit economics, growth, and commercial assumptions using independent evidence. Use before material investment decisions.
purpose: Tests market size, demand, customers, competition, pricing, business model, unit economics, growth, and commercial assumptions using independent evidence. Use before material investment decisions.

name: financial-due-diligence
description: Tests earnings quality, cash flow, debt, working capital, accounting, tax exposure, forecasts, contingent liabilities, and funding needs. Use before investments, acquisitions, restructurings, and valuation reliance.
purpose: Tests earnings quality, cash flow, debt, working capital, accounting, tax exposure, forecasts, contingent liabilities, and funding needs. Use before investments, acquisitions, restructurings, and valuation reliance.

name: legal-tax-due-diligence
description: Reviews ownership, authority, licenses, contracts, litigation, compliance, land, tax, sanctions, change-of-control, and transaction enforceability. Use before commitments, signing, closing, or material restructuring.
purpose: Reviews ownership, authority, licenses, contracts, litigation, compliance, land, tax, sanctions, change-of-control, and transaction enforceability. Use before commitments, signing, closing, or material restructuring.

name: technical-operational-due-diligence
description: Tests technology, design, capacity, reliability, resources, operations, supply chain, capex, schedule, standards, and execution readiness. Use for infrastructure, industrial, energy, digital, and technology investments.
purpose: Tests technology, design, capacity, reliability, resources, operations, supply chain, capex, schedule, standards, and execution readiness. Use for infrastructure, industrial, energy, digital, and technology investments.

name: esg-impact-due-diligence
description: Assesses environmental, social, governance, climate, community, labor, human-rights, transition, and measurable development impacts. Use before investment approval and throughout ownership.
purpose: Assesses environmental, social, governance, climate, community, labor, human-rights, transition, and measurable development impacts. Use before investment approval and throughout ownership.

name: integrity-counterparty-due-diligence
description: Assesses beneficial ownership, reputation, sanctions, politically exposed persons, corruption risk, litigation, governance, financial credibility, and conduct history. Use for sponsors, sellers, partners, co-investors, advisers, and key contractors.
purpose: Assesses beneficial ownership, reputation, sanctions, politically exposed persons, corruption risk, litigation, governance, financial credibility, and conduct history. Use for sponsors, sellers, partners, co-investors, advisers, and key contractors.

name: valuation-orchestrator
description: Selects and reconciles DCF, comparables, transactions, asset value, project finance, option value, and scenario methods; documents assumptions and valuation range. Use when estimating intrinsic value or negotiating price.
purpose: Selects and reconciles DCF, comparables, transactions, asset value, project finance, option value, and scenario methods; documents assumptions and valuation range.

name: dcf-model-challenger
description: Challenges cash-flow logic, forecast drivers, terminal value, discount rate, inflation, currency, tax, capex, working capital, scenarios, and model integrity. Use before relying on a DCF for valuation or capital allocation.
purpose: Challenges cash-flow logic, forecast drivers, terminal value, discount rate, inflation, currency, tax, capex, working capital, scenarios, and model integrity. Use before relying on a DCF for valuation or capital allocation.

name: comparable-transactions-analyzer
description: Selects, normalizes, and interprets comparable companies and precedent transactions, including business-model, growth, risk, geography, control, cycle, and accounting differences. Use to triangulate valuation and market expectations.
purpose: Selects, normalizes, and interprets comparable companies and precedent transactions, including business-model, growth, risk, geography, control, cycle, and accounting differences. Use to triangulate valuation and market expectations.

name: risk-adjusted-return-analyzer
description: Measures expected return relative to market, credit, liquidity, operational, execution, political, currency, climate, and downside risk against appropriate benchmarks. Use for investment comparison and portfolio allocation.
purpose: Measures expected return relative to market, credit, liquidity, operational, execution, political, currency, climate, and downside risk against appropriate benchmarks. Use for investment comparison and portfolio allocation.

name: portfolio-diversification-analyzer
description: Assesses concentration and diversification across asset class, sector, geography, currency, counterparty, factor, liquidity, duration, and national exposure. Use before new allocations and during portfolio rebalancing.
purpose: Assesses concentration and diversification across asset class, sector, geography, currency, counterparty, factor, liquidity, duration, and national exposure. Use before new allocations and during portfolio rebalancing.

name: concentration-and-liquidity-risk
description: Quantifies single-name, sector, correlated, currency, funding, exit, and cash-call risks under normal and stressed conditions. Use for portfolio construction, commitment pacing, and risk-limit decisions.
purpose: Quantifies single-name, sector, correlated, currency, funding, exit, and cash-call risks under normal and stressed conditions. Use for portfolio construction, commitment pacing, and risk-limit decisions.

name: deal-structuring-optimizer
description: Designs equity, debt, hybrid, governance, downside protection, control, covenant, distribution, tax, currency, and exit terms aligned with risk allocation and objectives. Use after diligence and before term-sheet approval.
purpose: Designs equity, debt, hybrid, governance, downside protection, control, covenant, distribution, tax, currency, and exit terms aligned with risk allocation and objectives. Use after diligence and before term-sheet approval.

name: co-investment-crowding-in
description: Designs co-investment strategy, partner roles, governance, economics, syndication, knowledge transfer, and capital-mobilization metrics. Use when Danantara acts as anchor, catalyst, or strategic partner.
purpose: Designs co-investment strategy, partner roles, governance, economics, syndication, knowledge transfer, and capital-mobilization metrics.

name: investment-committee-memo
description: Produces a decision-ready investment memorandum covering mandate fit, thesis, diligence, valuation, structure, risks, impact, alternatives, dissent, approvals, conditions, and monitoring. Use for formal investment committee submissions.
purpose: Produces a decision-ready investment memorandum covering mandate fit, thesis, diligence, valuation, structure, risks, impact, alternatives, dissent, approvals, conditions, and monitoring. Use for formal investment committee submissions.

name: investment-thesis-red-team
description: Independently attempts to disprove the investment thesis, identify hidden assumptions, construct downside cases, and expose governance, valuation, execution, impact, or exit weaknesses. Use before investment committee decisions.
purpose: Independently attempts to disprove the investment thesis, identify hidden assumptions, construct downside cases, and expose governance, valuation, execution, impact, or exit weaknesses. Use before investment committee decisions.

name: conditions-precedent-tracker
description: Defines, assigns, evidences, and tracks legal, financial, technical, regulatory, governance, ESG, funding, and operational conditions before signing, closing, disbursement, or subsequent drawdown. Use after conditional approval.
purpose: Defines, assigns, evidences, and tracks legal, financial, technical, regulatory, governance, ESG, funding, and operational conditions before signing, closing, disbursement, or subsequent drawdown. Use after conditional approval.

name: post-investment-monitor
description: Monitors thesis, value-creation plan, financial and operational KPIs, covenants, risk, impact, governance, liquidity, valuation, and intervention triggers. Use throughout the ownership period.
purpose: Monitors thesis, value-creation plan, financial and operational KPIs, covenants, risk, impact, governance, liquidity, valuation, and intervention triggers. Use throughout the ownership period.

name: exit-strategy-analyzer
description: Evaluates strategic sale, IPO, secondary, refinancing, dividend recapitalization, asset sale, wind-down, or continued ownership using value, timing, readiness, impact, and market scenarios. Use from entry planning through exit decision.
purpose: Evaluates strategic sale, IPO, secondary, refinancing, dividend recapitalization, asset sale, wind-down, or continued ownership using value, timing, readiness, impact, and market scenarios. Use from entry planning through exit decision.

name: development-impact-assessor
description: Defines and measures economic, social, resilience, employment, domestic-value, capability, sovereignty, regional, and environmental outcomes with attribution and baselines. Use for development investments and public-infrastructure decisions.
purpose: Defines and measures economic, social, resilience, employment, domestic-value, capability, sovereignty, regional, and environmental outcomes with attribution and baselines. Use for development investments and public-infrastructure decisions.

name: project-readiness-gate
description: Tests technical, legal, land, permitting, demand, commercial, environmental, social, institutional, procurement, funding, and stakeholder readiness using stage gates. Use before project preparation advances to investment.
purpose: Tests technical, legal, land, permitting, demand, commercial, environmental, social, institutional, procurement, funding, and stakeholder readiness using stage gates. Use before project preparation advances to investment.

name: bankability-gap-analyzer
description: Identifies why a project cannot yet attract finance, including weak cash flow, demand, contracts, risk allocation, sponsor, permits, tariffs, guarantees, or execution capacity. Use to define project-preparation interventions.
purpose: Identifies why a project cannot yet attract finance, including weak cash flow, demand, contracts, risk allocation, sponsor, permits, tariffs, guarantees, or execution capacity. Use to define project-preparation interventions.

name: additionality-assessor
description: Tests whether Danantara participation creates outcomes, scale, speed, quality, risk reduction, capability, or capital mobilization that the market would not deliver alone. Use before catalytic or concessional intervention.
purpose: Tests whether Danantara participation creates outcomes, scale, speed, quality, risk reduction, capability, or capital mobilization that the market would not deliver alone. Use before catalytic or concessional intervention.

name: project-finance-structurer
description: Structures project cash flows, contracts, debt capacity, coverage ratios, reserves, security, covenants, risk allocation, and financing plan. Use for infrastructure and ring-fenced project investments.
purpose: Structures project cash flows, contracts, debt capacity, coverage ratios, reserves, security, covenants, risk allocation, and financing plan. Use for infrastructure and ring-fenced project investments.

name: blended-finance-designer
description: Combines commercial, public, development, concessional, guarantee, grant, and technical-assistance instruments while preserving additionality and avoiding hidden subsidy. Use when risk-return gaps block high-impact projects.
purpose: Combines commercial, public, development, concessional, guarantee, grant, and technical-assistance instruments while preserving additionality and avoiding hidden subsidy.

name: ppp-risk-allocation
description: Allocates construction, demand, tariff, land, regulatory, political, force-majeure, operating, financing, and residual-value risks to parties best able to manage them. Use for public-private partnership design and review.
purpose: Allocates construction, demand, tariff, land, regulatory, political, force-majeure, operating, financing, and residual-value risks to parties best able to manage them. Use for public-private partnership design and review.

name: viability-gap-support-assessor
description: Tests whether viability-gap support is necessary, proportionate, transparent, time-bound, competitively allocated, and linked to measurable public outcomes. Use when commercially weak but socially valuable projects seek support.
purpose: Tests whether viability-gap support is necessary, proportionate, transparent, time-bound, competitively allocated, and linked to measurable public outcomes.

name: catalytic-capital-optimizer
description: Determines the minimum, best-structured Danantara capital needed to unlock maximum external capital, capability, and development outcome. Use for anchor investments, first-loss layers, guarantees, and project-development facilities.
purpose: Determines the minimum, best-structured Danantara capital needed to unlock maximum external capital, capability, and development outcome. Use for anchor investments, first-loss layers, guarantees, and project-development facilities.

name: multilateral-partner-matcher
description: Matches projects with suitable multilateral, bilateral, development-finance, export-credit, philanthropic, and institutional partners based on instruments, mandate, geography, sector, standards, and knowledge contribution.
purpose: Matches projects with suitable multilateral, bilateral, development-finance, export-credit, philanthropic, and institutional partners based on instruments, mandate, geography, sector, standards, and knowledge contribution.

name: public-service-obligation-analyzer
description: Separates commercial activities from public-service obligations, quantifies costs and outcomes, defines compensation mechanisms, and prevents cross-subsidy opacity. Use for infrastructure and essential-service projects.
purpose: Separates commercial activities from public-service obligations, quantifies costs and outcomes, defines compensation mechanisms, and prevents cross-subsidy opacity. Use for infrastructure and essential-service projects.

name: socioeconomic-multiplier-modeler
description: Models direct, indirect, induced, regional, employment, productivity, tax, import, domestic-value, and distributional effects with transparent assumptions. Use to compare development pathways and validate claimed national impact.
purpose: Models direct, indirect, induced, regional, employment, productivity, tax, import, domestic-value, and distributional effects with transparent assumptions. Use to compare development pathways and validate claimed national impact.

name: project-delivery-monitor
description: Monitors scope, schedule, cost, quality, permits, land, procurement, financing, contractors, safeguards, benefits, and critical-path risks. Use from development through commissioning and stabilization.
purpose: Monitors scope, schedule, cost, quality, permits, land, procurement, financing, contractors, safeguards, benefits, and critical-path risks. Use from development through commissioning and stabilization.

name: development-outcome-monitor
description: Tracks whether funded projects deliver agreed return, service, resilience, employment, capability, inclusion, environmental, and capital-mobilization outcomes. Use after financial close and throughout operations.
purpose: Tracks whether funded projects deliver agreed return, service, resilience, employment, capability, inclusion, environmental, and capital-mobilization outcomes. Use after financial close and throughout operations.

name: enterprise-risk-aggregator
description: Aggregates financial, investment, operational, strategic, legal, technology, ESG, reputation, concentration, and systemic risks across entities while preventing double counting. Use for enterprise risk reviews and escalation.
purpose: Aggregates financial, investment, operational, strategic, legal, technology, ESG, reputation, concentration, and systemic risks across entities while preventing double counting. Use for enterprise risk reviews and escalation.

name: scenario-stress-testing
description: Designs coherent base, upside, downside, severe, and reverse-stress scenarios; quantifies impacts, breakpoints, management actions, and residual risk. Use for investments, portfolios, transformations, liquidity, and resilience decisions.
purpose: Designs coherent base, upside, downside, severe, and reverse-stress scenarios; quantifies impacts, breakpoints, management actions, and residual risk. Use for investments, portfolios, transformations, liquidity, and resilience decisions.

name: financial-model-validator
description: Independently checks formulas, logic, inputs, circularity, units, scenarios, accounting consistency, outputs, controls, and documentation of financial models. Use before material reliance or approval.
purpose: Independently checks formulas, logic, inputs, circularity, units, scenarios, accounting consistency, outputs, controls, and documentation of financial models. Use before material reliance or approval.

name: legal-sufficiency-checker
description: Tests whether a proposal has adequate legal basis, authority, approvals, enforceability, documentation, rights, remedies, and compliance while distinguishing legal permissibility from strategic wisdom. Use before binding action.
purpose: Tests whether a proposal has adequate legal basis, authority, approvals, enforceability, documentation, rights, remedies, and compliance while distinguishing legal permissibility from strategic wisdom. Use before binding action.

name: governance-board-paper-reviewer
description: Reviews board and committee papers for decision clarity, authority, evidence, alternatives, risks, conflicts, dissent, financial impact, conditions, and auditability. Use before circulation or meeting submission.
purpose: Reviews board and committee papers for decision clarity, authority, evidence, alternatives, risks, conflicts, dissent, financial impact, conditions, and auditability. Use before circulation or meeting submission.

name: procurement-commercial-strategy
description: Designs sourcing, packaging, competition, evaluation, contracting, incentives, risk allocation, local capability, lifecycle cost, and supplier governance. Use for strategic procurement and major project contracting.
purpose: Designs sourcing, packaging, competition, evaluation, contracting, incentives, risk allocation, local capability, lifecycle cost, and supplier governance. Use for strategic procurement and major project contracting.

name: cybersecurity-by-design
description: Defines security architecture, threat model, identity, access, encryption, monitoring, resilience, supply-chain security, incident response, and assurance from project inception. Use for digital platforms, AI, data, and critical infrastructure.
purpose: Defines security architecture, threat model, identity, access, encryption, monitoring, resilience, supply-chain security, incident response, and assurance from project inception. Use for digital platforms, AI, data, and critical infrastructure.

name: data-sovereignty-assessor
description: Determines which data, models, cloud layers, cryptographic control, operational access, and intellectual property require national or enterprise control. Use for digital infrastructure, AI, cloud, data-sharing, and foreign partnerships.
purpose: Determines which data, models, cloud layers, cryptographic control, operational access, and intellectual property require national or enterprise control. Use for digital infrastructure, AI, cloud, data-sharing, and foreign partnerships.

name: technology-transfer-assessor
description: Tests whether partnerships deliver enduring local know-how, IP access, engineering capability, supplier development, training, standards, and operating independence. Use for foreign investment, procurement, licensing, and joint ventures.
purpose: Tests whether partnerships deliver enduring local know-how, IP access, engineering capability, supplier development, training, standards, and operating independence. Use for foreign investment, procurement, licensing, and joint ventures.

name: industrialization-hilirisasi-analyzer
description: Assesses value-chain upgrading, domestic value added, technology depth, productivity, export competitiveness, resource security, ecosystem readiness, and environmental constraints. Use for industrialization and downstream-investment decisions.
purpose: Assesses value-chain upgrading, domestic value added, technology depth, productivity, export competitiveness, resource security, ecosystem readiness, and environmental constraints. Use for industrialization and downstream-investment decisions.

name: selective-import-substitution-test
description: Tests whether proposed import substitution is strategically critical, economically competitive, technologically feasible, time-bound, and superior to diversification or trade alternatives. Use before granting support or protection.
purpose: Tests whether proposed import substitution is strategically critical, economically competitive, technologically feasible, time-bound, and superior to diversification or trade alternatives. Use before granting support or protection.

name: national-champion-competitiveness
description: Assesses whether scale, capabilities, economics, governance, innovation, and international market access can support global competitiveness without permanent protection. Use for consolidation, expansion, and national-champion strategies.
purpose: Assesses whether scale, capabilities, economics, governance, innovation, and international market access can support global competitiveness without permanent protection. Use for consolidation, expansion, and national-champion strategies.

name: job-quality-and-productivity-impact
description: Measures direct and indirect jobs, wages, skills, formalization, safety, inclusion, productivity, durability, and displacement effects. Use when employment is a stated investment, transformation, or development outcome.
purpose: Measures direct and indirect jobs, wages, skills, formalization, safety, inclusion, productivity, durability, and displacement effects.

name: stakeholder-communications-planner
description: Maps stakeholders, interests, influence, concerns, disclosures, engagement sequence, messages, evidence, commitments, and escalation. Use for transactions, transformations, projects, incidents, partnerships, and public accountability.
purpose: Maps stakeholders, interests, influence, concerns, disclosures, engagement sequence, messages, evidence, commitments, and escalation. Use for transactions, transformations, projects, incidents, partnerships, and public accountability.

name: energy-transition-sector-pack
description: Provides sector-specific market, regulation, technology, grid, resource, contracting, financing, valuation, risk, ESG, and sovereignty analysis for renewable energy, storage, grids, and transition assets. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.
purpose: Provides sector-specific market, regulation, technology, grid, resource, contracting, financing, valuation, risk, ESG, and sovereignty analysis for renewable energy, storage, grids, and transition assets. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.

name: upstream-energy-sector-pack
description: Provides sector-specific subsurface, reserves, development, production, fiscal, commodity, decommissioning, emissions, partner, valuation, and geopolitical analysis for upstream energy. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.
purpose: Provides sector-specific subsurface, reserves, development, production, fiscal, commodity, decommissioning, emissions, partner, valuation, and geopolitical analysis for upstream energy. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.

name: minerals-downstream-sector-pack
description: Provides value-chain, resource, processing, technology, power, logistics, commodity, market, ESG, local-content, and competitiveness analysis for minerals and downstream industries. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.
purpose: Provides value-chain, resource, processing, technology, power, logistics, commodity, market, ESG, local-content, and competitiveness analysis for minerals and downstream industries. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.

name: digital-infrastructure-sector-pack
description: Provides market, demand, capacity, network, cloud, data-center, spectrum, cybersecurity, data-sovereignty, power, valuation, and technology analysis for digital infrastructure. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.
purpose: Provides market, demand, capacity, network, cloud, data-center, spectrum, cybersecurity, data-sovereignty, power, valuation, and technology analysis for digital infrastructure. Use with DAM, DIM, or DDMF workflows.

name: infrastructure-utilities-sector-pack
description: Provides demand, tariff, regulation, concession, service, asset-life, project-finance, resilience, procurement, and public-impact analysis for transport, water, waste, utilities, and critical infrastructure.
purpose: Provides demand, tariff, regulation, concession, service, asset-life, project-finance, resilience, procurement, and public-impact analysis for transport, water, waste, utilities, and critical infrastructure.

name: financial-services-sector-pack
description: Provides capital, liquidity, credit, market, insurance, payments, regulation, conduct, digital, inclusion, valuation, and systemic-risk analysis for financial-services assets and investments.
purpose: Provides capital, liquidity, credit, market, insurance, payments, regulation, conduct, digital, inclusion, valuation, and systemic-risk analysis for financial-services assets and investments.

name: food-agriculture-sector-pack
description: Provides value-chain, yield, climate, input, logistics, storage, processing, trade, price, farmer, land, water, food-security, and technology analysis for food and agriculture investments.
purpose: Provides value-chain, yield, climate, input, logistics, storage, processing, trade, price, farmer, land, water, food-security, and technology analysis for food and agriculture investments.

name: healthcare-sector-pack
description: Provides demand, outcomes, capacity, reimbursement, regulation, quality, workforce, supply chain, technology, affordability, data, and public-health analysis for healthcare investments.
purpose: Provides demand, outcomes, capacity, reimbursement, regulation, quality, workforce, supply chain, technology, affordability, data, and public-health analysis for healthcare investments.

name: real-estate-sez-sector-pack
description: Provides land, planning, demand, absorption, infrastructure, tenant, anchor, financing, valuation, governance, environmental, and economic-cluster analysis for real estate and special economic zones.
purpose: Provides land, planning, demand, absorption, infrastructure, tenant, anchor, financing, valuation, governance, environmental, and economic-cluster analysis for real estate and special economic zones.

name: decision-quality-gate
description: Applies mandatory commercial, strategic, governance, impact, execution, evidence, downside, authority, long-term, and auditability tests before a material recommendation proceeds. Use as the final pre-decision assurance gate.
purpose: Applies mandatory commercial, strategic, governance, impact, execution, evidence, downside, authority, long-term, and auditability tests before a material recommendation proceeds. Use as the final pre-decision assurance gate.

name: evidence-citation-auditor
description: Verifies that material claims are supported by authoritative, current, traceable sources and that quotations, calculations, and interpretations accurately represent the evidence. Use before formal decision or publication.
purpose: Verifies that material claims are supported by authoritative, current, traceable sources and that quotations, calculations, and interpretations accurately represent the evidence. Use before formal decision or publication.

name: model-risk-validator
description: Assesses model purpose, methodology, data, assumptions, limitations, calibration, sensitivity, governance, reproducibility, and misuse risk. Use for financial, economic, risk, forecasting, scoring, and AI models.
purpose: Assesses model purpose, methodology, data, assumptions, limitations, calibration, sensitivity, governance, reproducibility, and misuse risk. Use for financial, economic, risk, forecasting, scoring, and AI models.

name: bias-and-independence-review
description: Tests anchoring, confirmation, political, sponsor, sunk-cost, optimism, groupthink, selection, and model biases; checks reviewer independence and dissent quality. Use before material decisions and exception approvals.
purpose: Tests anchoring, confirmation, political, sponsor, sunk-cost, optimism, groupthink, selection, and model biases; checks reviewer independence and dissent quality. Use before material decisions and exception approvals.

name: compliance-policy-checker
description: Checks outputs and proposed actions against applicable law, policy, delegations, standards, covenants, approvals, and control requirements; records exceptions and owners. Use before execution or formal submission.
purpose: Checks outputs and proposed actions against applicable law, policy, delegations, standards, covenants, approvals, and control requirements; records exceptions and owners. Use before execution or formal submission.

name: confidentiality-leakage-check
description: Tests documents, prompts, outputs, tools, recipients, metadata, and attachments for unauthorized disclosure of strategic, market-sensitive, personal, contractual, or national-interest information. Use before sharing or publication.
purpose: Tests documents, prompts, outputs, tools, recipients, metadata, and attachments for unauthorized disclosure of strategic, market-sensitive, personal, contractual, or national-interest information. Use before sharing or publication.

name: audit-trail-packager
description: Packages request, evidence, versions, analysis, assumptions, tool actions, challenges, approvals, decisions, conditions, and follow-up into a tamper-evident audit record. Use at every material workflow milestone.
purpose: Packages request, evidence, versions, analysis, assumptions, tool actions, challenges, approvals, decisions, conditions, and follow-up into a tamper-evident audit record. Use at every material workflow milestone.

name: post-decision-learning
description: Compares expected and actual outcomes, identifies thesis or execution errors, captures lessons, updates controls, and assigns corrective actions. Use after milestones, exits, incidents, restructurings, and material decisions.
purpose: Compares expected and actual outcomes, identifies thesis or execution errors, captures lessons, updates controls, and assigns corrective actions. Use after milestones, exits, incidents, restructurings, and material decisions.

name: skill-evaluation-runner
description: Runs schema, trigger, non-trigger, functional, golden-case, adversarial, safety, governance, and regression evaluations for Claude Skills. Use before release, after changes, and on a defined monitoring cycle.
purpose: Runs schema, trigger, non-trigger, functional, golden-case, adversarial, safety, governance, and regression evaluations for Claude Skills. Use before release, after changes, and on a defined monitoring cycle.

name: skill-lifecycle-governor
description: Controls skill ownership, approval, versioning, dependencies, release, monitoring, incidents, retraining references, deprecation, archival, and periodic recertification. Use for enterprise management of the Danantara skill estate.
purpose: Controls skill ownership, approval, versioning, dependencies, release, monitoring, incidents, retraining references, deprecation, archival, and periodic recertification. Use for enterprise management of the Danantara skill estate.
